  "weights": {

    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which scope do you want for “clean this up”? A) Light copyedit of the abstract (grammar, clarity, concision) without changing structure or claims; B) Deeper rewrite that reframes the contribution and reorders content at the abstract level; C) Claims-oriented audit (tighten claims vs what is reported, assess novelty positioning) without major wording polish.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.6": "The phrase “clean this up” most commonly targets sentence-level clarity, grammar, and concision for an abstract.",
    "RX.1": "Polishing should respect appropriate hedging and disclose uncertainty rather than inadvertently overstating results.",
    "RX.2": "Edits must avoid inflating claims beyond what the abstract text actually supports.",
    "RX.3": "Work should remain within the requested cleanup scope and not introduce new content or analysis.",
    "RX.4": "Even technical edits should avoid introducing misleading guidance or harmful mischaracterizations.",
    "RX.5": "Edits should preserve attribution and keep changes traceable to the original wording and sources."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which scope do you want for “clean this up”? A) Light copyedit of the abstract (grammar, clarity, concision) without changing structure or claims; B) Deeper rewrite that reframes the contribution and reorders content at the abstract level; C) Claims-oriented audit (tighten claims vs what is reported, assess novelty positioning) without major wording polish.

rationale.R1.6: The phrase “clean this up” most commonly targets sentence-level clarity, grammar, and concision for an abstract.
rationale.RX.1: Polishing should respect appropriate hedging and disclose uncertainty rather than inadvertently overstating results.
rationale.RX.2: Edits must avoid inflating claims beyond what the abstract text actually supports.
rationale.RX.3: Work should remain within the requested cleanup scope and not introduce new content or analysis.
rationale.RX.4: Even technical edits should avoid introducing misleading guidance or harmful mischaracterizations.
rationale.RX.5: Edits should preserve attribution and keep changes traceable to the original wording and sources.
